evidence: TASK-033 verified end-to-end; the last FEAT-007 slice. Criterion 1: samples/conception.json declares the two governance relation_types (item_governed_by_convention: decisions/features/tasks -> conventions; item_acknowledges_missing_convention: decisions/features/tasks -> framework-gaps) and the three requires-edge invariants (decision/feature/task-articulates-convention) at severity warning, byte-identical to the .context copies but for severity (merged to integration at 1383af2). Criterion 2: a fresh /tmp substrate built through the genuine CLI bootstrap (context-init -> context-accept-all -> context-install) carries the vocabulary at severity warning (read-config confirmed), and an un-sentineled unarticulated task appended there yields context-validate status 'warnings' with the task-articulates-convention WARNING (not error) on that item — run twice (the convergent e2e). Criterion 3: CHANGELOG [Unreleased] + skill-narrative + READMEs updated (usage-only); build + check + full test fail 0; parity-check exit 0; SKILL regen carries the advisory-convention note. Criterion 4: released v0.31.0 via npm run release:minor — lockstep version bump across all packages, the surface gate green (after the release.mjs learned-pattern-store exemption fix 4425ff2), commit 307199a + tag v0.31.0; npm publish + git push remain the user's downstream steps. The release also cut the accumulated [Unreleased] (read-catalog-schema/TASK-050, context-install/TASK-059, the governance vocab/TASK-033, the earlier FEAT-009 ops) into [0.31.0]. The mechanism is config-only — no validator code change (FEAT-007 criterion 3).
